entries.title: Classified original model as Layer (L2), not Model (L3)
entries.description: The original 8_ResNetBasicBlock.py defines a single ResNet BasicBlock, which is a building block (Layer, L2), not a full model (L3). The decomposition starts at L2 and goes to L1 fusions and L0 kernels.

entries.title: Decomposed into 3 fusions + 1 standalone kernel
entries.description: The BasicBlock decomposes into: (1) conv_bn_relu fusion (conv1+bn1+relu, straight-line computation), (2) conv_bn fusion (conv2+bn2, straight-line), (3) downsample fusion (conv1x1+bn, straight-line). The final ReLU after residual add is a standalone kernel (L0) because it is isolated by the residual add operation.

entries.title: ReLU in conv_bn_relu fusion vs standalone final ReLU
entries.description: The first ReLU is part of the conv_bn_relu fusion because it is adjacent to conv1+bn1 with no data flow break. The second ReLU (after residual add) is standalone because the residual add breaks the fusion opportunity. It is classified as L0 kernel.

entries.title: Kernel deduplication: batchnorm2d_64_fp32 shared by 3 instances
entries.description: All three BatchNorm2d operations (bn1, bn2, downsample.1) have identical shape (64 channels). They share a single kernel file batchnorm2d_64_fp32.py with instance_count=3.

entries.title: Kernel deduplication: relu_fp32 shared by 2 instances
entries.description: ReLU is shape-independent (elementwise). Two instances exist: one inside conv_bn_relu fusion and one standalone after residual add. Both share relu_fp32.py with instance_count=2.

entries.title: Three distinct Conv2d kernels due to different shapes
entries.description: conv1 (3->64, 3x3), conv2 (64->64, 3x3), and downsample conv (3->64, 1x1) are different workloads due to different parameter shapes. They get separate kernel files: conv2d_3x64_3x3_fp32.py, conv2d_64x64_3x3_fp32.py, conv2d_3x64_1x1_fp32.py.
